title: April 2024
excerpt: Learning Java and the beginning of the journey.
content: My plan at the time was to become good in my profession, software engineering.

title: October 2024
excerpt: Building a chess engine and wanting projects that tell a story.
content:
I started reading a Java book.

After learning the fundamentals, and exploring a framework at work, I decided to make a personal project. Build up a portfolio.

Since I enjoy chess, I started building a chess engine.

In the following months, I spent some time after work on it and I began to find the project more and more interesting.

title: February 2025
excerpt: Turning the chess engine into a full chess web application.
content:
I wanted people to experience what I was working on.

I decided to turn the chess engine, previously run locally, into a full chess web application, SKGCHESS.

I spend the next months building it with great enthusiasm.

title: April 2025
excerpt: First move over websockets, UI, and learning that appearance and story matter.
content:
For the first time, I had a move sent and displayed to the other player.

Next I wanted to shape the app's appearance.

This part took much longer than I expected. I wanted visiting the site to create a specific narrative, expressed through the content, colors, music, and the overall presentation.

I realized that I am more interested in the user experiencing what I create as a story than in the architecture of the application. Moreover, I understood that most professional software exists to create profit. This made me reflect on my work as a software engineer.

Although I am very proud of it, respect it as a field, and I will always enjoy working with people who are committed to producing exceptional work, I realized that I have a need to create things that have meaning beyond myself. Things that are created with the purpose of touching on broader ideas, raising questions about social or human matters, and inviting reflection, rather than being constrained to create value for a business.

I continued refining SKGCHESS to match the way I had imagined it, until finally stepping away from it in June.

title: June 2025
excerpt: Two paths (game development or freelancing), first game concept, and the chess club email.
content:
Having understood that I'm more interested in creating narratives, I started thinking in ways that I could view my work from this perspective and create something that people would want to be more invested.

I thought of two ways:

  • Create a game
  • Start freelancing (indie projects).

I decided to try game development first.

Since I know how difficult and time consuming it is, I first wanted to validate the reasons behind choosing to create a game.

NOTE: Before June 2025, I had already tried to play with a game engine, the first time being in winter in 2023, where I just messed up with things, and the second in February of 2025 while developing SKGCHESS, where I spend a few days modeling on Blender. It's worth noting that the model of the dog appearing in the front page, was made during those days. It is my dog Lilith, as you can see here :)

(image of lilith)

This time I wanted to find out if I have a story to tell.

I wrote down an idea for a scenario. Very roughly: you are inside the mind of a protagonist who is in a state of dormancy, and you are asked to explore its different regions, unlock their lost emotions and wake them up from numbness.

Next, I started thinking about gameplay mechanics around this idea.

I found myself drawn in completely, exploring how I could create something thought-provoking, handling existential questions, while also being fun to play.

I thought it would be wise to understand what game design is.

I read a book named "A Theory of Fun for Game Design" by Raph Koster.

I took away many interesting insights and continued thinking through what I want to create.

End June 2025

I received an email about SKGCHESS. I had posted it on social media some time ago, and a friend from work approached me to help.

The whole discussion pushed me to return to the project again and refine it further before posting it on the website of one of the few local chess clubs in my hometown, Thessaloniki.

With knowing that the project will reach more people, I decided to create a personal page.

This would also help me explore freelancing, the other idea I had in mind to follow as a path.

I spent the next months, trying to shape how this personal space would look, while also refining SKGCHESS.

title: September 2025
excerpt: Taking a step back, Unreal Engine, painting, Blender, FL Studio, and the big questions.
content:
I went on vacation and had some time to reflect.

Coming back, I decided to leave aside both SKGCHESS and the idea of freelancing for now.

I have spent more than a year in what felt like inner searching, and I decided I needed to take a step back.

I spent the next months enjoying life and trying to calm down.

title: November 2025
excerpt: T a step back, Unreal Engine, painting, Blender, FL Studio, and the big questions.
content:
I returned to the idea of making a game.

Since I had already explored a story to tell, understood what a game designer does, and experimented with Blender earlier, I started looking into technical decisions

.

I explored what would it mean to create a game in 3D.

I opened up the most capable 3D engine, created some landscapes and experimented with modeling.

This sparked a broader curiosity about art in general.

I wanted to learn more about art and figure out whether I could achieve what I imagined with going 2D, thus avoiding the complexities of 3D.

I began painting for the first time and read a book gifted to me by a good friend, The Story of Art by Gombrich. I also started to search for art styles I can use.

Next, I went on to explore creating music for my game, as this would help better understand the broader picture.

Over the following months, I engaged with these activities from time to time, and I frequently returned to reevaluate the game development idea.

title: January 2026
excerpt: Writing the complete story and the world document.
content:
After some time, I now knew I wanted to create and was committed to pursuing it.

Now, to investigate the game development idea seriously, I came back to what I did in June with the scenario attempt and this time I tried harder. I wrote a complete story, a mixed version of my first scenario, along with new direction. I made a document with the entire history of the world: how it could be delivered narratively, what type of game could help achieve the message, and how the gameplay mechanics could connect with the story of the world.

title: February 2026
excerpt: Switching to open source and looking into Godot.
content: After moving away from Spotify, I decided more broadly that I want to avoid proprietary software. I switched fully to open source. I reinstalled Linux (which I had used as a student), purged Windows, and with it the Shadow PC service as well. With this shift, I decided that if I were to make a game, it should also be in an open source engine. So I started looking into Godot Engine.

title: March 2026
excerpt: Starting the game in Godot and Day 1 with the documentation.
content:
The journey of almost 1.5 years of constant searching feels like it is finally coming to an end. Along the way, I had many questions and explored finding meaning in many areas. I changed my perspective on things multiple times. I imagine that every few years in a person's life, something like this happens: a new perspective emerges, and a new chapter begins.

A few days ago, I finished IXLSTUDIO in the form you see now. Freelancing idea and the launch of SKGCHESS at the chess club, belongs to the past.

Recently, I started teaching my profession in a college in my hometown. My daily job continues to go well.

All the work is coming together.

I will make a game in Godot, and in 2D.

I will try to create my own art.

This is PROJECT MAKE A GAME.

Go to the journal to start following the documentation.
